items.title: What to Know About Shein's Plans for Mega HK IPO
items.description: Fast-fashion giant Shein is seeking to list in Hong Kong as soon as August, targeting to raise about $2 billion to $3 billion, sources say. Minmin Low reports on the company's fundamentals. (Source: B

items.title: Phillippine Peso Approaches Record Low as Oil Prices Advance
items.description: The Philippine peso falls toward a record low as escalating tensions in the Middle East drove oil prices higher, putting renewed pressure on the currency.

items.title: SK Hynix Shares Waver in Seoul After Wall Street AI Memory Rout
items.description: SK Hynix Inc. shares fluctuated in Seoul, reflecting the AI memory selloff on Wall Street prompting some buying into weakness.

items.title: Fed Rate-Hike Bets Mount Before Inflation Data, Warsh Testimony
items.description: Bond traders ramped up bets for a July interest-rate hike ahead of US inflation data and an appearance by the head of the Federal Reserve that stand to reinforce the need for action.

items.title: Gold Holds Decline on Renewed Hormuz Blockade and Hawkish Fed
items.description: Gold held a decline as escalating hostilities in the Strait of Hormuz and remarks by a US monetary policymaker raised the likelihood of interest-rate hikes to contain inflation.

items.title: Seidler Equity Is Looking to Sell Stake in 139-Year-Old Baseball Gear Maker Rawlings

items.title: Brazil’s Oncoclinicas Said to File for Out-of-Court Debt Rework
items.description: Oncoclinicas do Brasil Servicos Medicos SA is preparing to file for an out-of-court debt restructuring, which could come as soon as Monday night, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: Shein’s Slowing Growth Risks Weighing on Hong Kong IPO Valuation

items.title: India’s Beaten-Down Large Caps Set to Rebound on Foreign Flows
items.description: India’s large-cap stocks are set to narrow their underperformance to smaller peers, supported by the return of foreign investors, an improving earnings outlook and attractive valuations, according to

items.title: Gold Miners Cadillac, Amapa Minerals Add to Canada IPO Rebound
items.description: Cadillac Mines Corp. and Amapa Minerals Holdings Inc. filed for initial public offerings, in the latest sign that the pace of Canadian listings is starting to pick up.

items.title: Brent Climbs Past $85, Treasuries Extend Losses: Markets Wrap
items.description: Oil extended gains and Treasuries edged lower after the standoff between the US and Iran intensified, raising concern that supply disruptions will reignite inflation and strengthen the case for higher

items.title: Oil Tops $85 a Barrel as Trump Reinstates Naval Blockade on Iran

items.title: Russian Billionaire Wins Fight to Boot Manager of Biotech Fund
items.description: Russian billionaire Dmitry Rybolovlev’s family trust prevailed in its effort to remove the manager of a bankrupt $2.5 billion venture capital fund it backed.

items.title: Argentina IPO Pipeline Grows as YPF Power Unit Files For US Listing
items.description: The power-generation unit of Argentina’s state-run energy firm YPF SA has filed for a US initial public offering, joining a growing pipeline of Argentine companies seeking New York listings.

items.title: Afore Banamex Chief Investment Officer Acosta to Leave Firm
items.description: The chief investment officer of Mexican pension fund Afore Banamex, Tomas Acosta, is leaving the firm, according to people familiar with the matter.
